summary: Deprecated audit trail for the old suggestions bundle. The ContentDeliveryManager IDs are still observed, but the parent record is no longer the primary research surface.

setting.name: Content Delivery Manager Suggestions Bundle
setting.casual_explanation: This bundle turns off several Windows tips, recommendations, and suggestion-style entries tied to Content Delivery Manager.
setting.why_people_change_it: People change it when they want a quieter Windows experience with fewer suggestions and soft-promoted content.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: The effect is usually desirable for power users, but the individual ContentDeliveryManager IDs are still opaque enough that this bundle is kept as review-only research.
setting.professional_notes: The provider writes five current-user SubscribedContent-* values to 0 under ContentDeliveryManager. This is treated as an observed bundle rather than a validated official control surface.

allowed_values.label: Windows default
allowed_values.meaning: Leave the current-user Content Delivery Manager suggestion IDs at their normal baseline.

allowed_values.label: Current app bundle
allowed_values.meaning: Apply the current set of suggestion-related ID values used by the app.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app writes five current-user suggestion-related ContentDeliveryManager values to 0.

states.rationale: Treat the numeric suggestion IDs as observed implementation details until they are better sourced.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
avoid_for: People explicitly testing the current app bundle

states.rationale: Do not publish the opaque ID bundle as a validated general-user toggle.

recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want the current product behavior for research.
tradeoffs: Less risk of mislabeling opaque IDs.

recommended_profiles.label: Current app bundle

states.rationale: Track the opaque ID bundle currently used by the app.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You are documenting the product's current suggestion-suppression behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a clean official mapping.
tradeoffs: Underlying ID semantics are still not clearly sourced.

evidence.title: Current privacy provider CDM suggestions bundle
evidence.summary: The provider writes five current-user ContentDeliveryManager suggestion-related values to 0.

decision.why: The bundle is useful to track, but the numeric suggestion IDs still need stronger semantic provenance.
blocking_issues: Opaque ContentDeliveryManager IDs are still observed implementation details rather than a clean official surface.
